key_insight: Universal ML interatomic potentials are best used as fast configuration-space generators, then relabeled with DFT to train material-specific models that achieve ab initio accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This workflow can cut the cost of generating reliable reactive-material trajectories, enabling near-DFT-quality simulations and model development in days rather than weeks.
method: Compare 5 universal MLIPs on 7 chemically diverse materials systems.
Use long MLIP-driven molecular dynamics to sample configurations, then sub-sample and relabel them with DFT.
Train or fine-tune material-specific MLIPs on the DFT-labeled dataset; for difficult cases, iterate self-training to expand coverage.
evidence_signal: Across the tested systems, about 2,000 DFT-recalculated structures were often enough for accurate models, and the hardest MoS2 case was recovered with only 600 total first-principles calculations.
watch_item: Zero-shot universal MLIPs can look strong on benchmarks yet still miss reactive, transport, and high-barrier processes, so DFT relabeling and iterative sampling remain essential for reliability.
paper_title: Universal Interatomic Potentials as Configuration-Space Generators for One-Shot and Iterative Fine-Tuning of Ab Initio-Accurate Material-Specific Models
